Lockheed Martin to quadruple Precision Strike Missile production

Quadrupling production capacity signals sustained U.S. military demand for precision strike capabilities, reshaping defense contractor supply chains and long-term aerospace manufacturing investment priorities.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Singapore Airlines to launch daily flights to new Western Sydney Airport

Singapore Airlines announced that it will begin daily nonstop service between Singapore’s Changi Airport (SIN) and the newly… The post Singapore Airlines to launch daily flights to new Western Sydney Airport appeared first on AeroTime.

Daily Singapore-Sydney service signals major carrier confidence in Western Sydney Airport's viability as a competitive long-haul hub despite Sydney's existing capacity constraints.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage

Swiss startup Pave Space has raised $40 million to develop an orbital transfer vehicle that could move satellites from LEO to their final destinations in hours instead of months. The post Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage appeared first on SpaceNews.

European autonomous orbital transfer vehicles could dramatically reduce satellite deployment costs by collapsing multi-month missions into hours, reshaping launch economics.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

What Are The Benefits Of Flying Emirates Business Class?

The benefits extend beyond the cabin and begin well before boarding.

Emirates' business class value proposition extends to ground services and airport experiences, creating competitive differentiation beyond traditional in-flight amenities.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations

NASA, concerned about slow development of commercial markets, is considering revamping its strategy for supporting the creation of commercial space stations to succeed the International Space Station. The post NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's shift signals recognition that market forces alone won't deliver orbital infrastructure on ISS retirement timeline, requiring direct agency intervention to ensure continuity.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective SRM requires organizations to systematically document control selection rationale and formally accept residual risks that cannot be eliminated, creating accountability beyond initial hazard identification.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Press Release: Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail partner on digital cabin

Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail have launched an integrated digital cabin ecosystem that unifies onboard retail, passenger and crew connectivity, and operational data into a single platform, marking a significant step forward in the digital transformation of Europe’s commercial aviation sector. ...

Integrated digital cabin platforms consolidate disparate systems into single ecosystems, reducing operational complexity while creating new ancillary revenue streams for cost-focused carriers.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds

Long-haul flights for families may become a little easier in the future after United Airlines unveiled plans to… The post United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds appeared first on AeroTime.

United's economy bed offering targets a high-margin family travel segment traditionally forced to upgrade, potentially reshaping long-haul revenue strategies across carriers.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 25, 2026

DOT&E Says No Combat-Capable TR-3-Configured F-35s Were Delivered in FY2025

The latest Director, Operational Test & Evaluation report says no combat-capable F-35 TR-3 aircraft have been delivered to the U.S. military until September 2025 because of the truncated software. The recently released 2025 Director, Operational Test & Evaluation (DOT&E) annual report brought ano...

F-35 TR-3 delivery delays due to incomplete software indicate sustained challenges in achieving full combat capability across the fighter fleet.

See The Aviationist →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

Wizz Air's digital cabin integration signals low-cost carriers are investing in ancillary revenue streams beyond seat sales to offset margin pressures.

Cross-border drone attacks on critical infrastructure demonstrate the expanding asymmetric threat to aviation security and civilian power systems in regional conflicts.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

5 Exciting Design Features Of The Airbus A321XLR's Cabin For Long-Haul Travel

Passengers on 'thinner' long-haul routes stand to benefit considerably from the A321XLR.

The A321XLR's cabin innovations enable profitably serving thin long-haul routes previously unviable for single-aisle aircraft, fundamentally expanding route networks for carriers.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9 depa

The Mustang 9 departure requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural constraints at Reno-Tahoe, suggesting potential terrain or traffic complexity that warrants enhanced briefing protocols.

Drone enters Latvian airspace from Russia, crashes near Kraslava

Latvia’s Air Force has identified a foreign unmanned aerial vehicle entering the country’s airspace from Russia before crashing… The post Drone enters Latvian airspace from Russia, crashes near Kraslava appeared first on AeroTime.

The incident underscores vulnerabilities in Eastern European airspace surveillance and the escalating use of unmanned systems as cross-border incursion tools during geopolitical tensions.

Lawmakers will have to queue alongside regular passengers.

Delta's removal of congressional flight perks signals growing airline willingness to challenge political relationships in favor of operational consistency and public perception management.

Military Mar 25, 2026

How The Northrop Grumman’s B-2 Spirit Cockpit Layout Prioritizes Long-Duration Mission Endurance

A closer look at the ergonomics of the world's first fifth-generation bomber.

The B-2's cockpit design optimizes crew comfort and fatigue management for missions exceeding 30 hours, establishing human factors as critical to stealth bomber operational effectiveness.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter accident

Wire strike accidents persist because pilots systematically overestimate their reaction capability, making low-altitude operations the deadliest phase of rotorcraft flight.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Couches In Economy: United Airlines Brings Premium To All Cabins With New Long-Haul Perk

UA's new Relax Row is rolling out in the main cabin this year.

United's Relax Row democratizes premium seating by offering economy passengers extra legroom seats at standard fares, potentially reshaping how carriers monetize cabin differentiation.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu

Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March fueled destructive flash flooding on several of the Hawaiian Islands.

Extreme weather events like consecutive subtropical cyclones can disrupt island aviation operations through runway closures, visibility reduction, and infrastructure damage affecting inter-island connectivity.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

How Much Does It Cost To Fly A Boeing 747-400 For 1 Hour?

The Boeing 747-400 is both more expensive and less expensive to operate than modern aircraft with fuel spikes adding to the cost range.

747-400 operating costs are highly volatile due to fuel price sensitivity, making budget predictability challenging compared to more efficient modern aircraft.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Optimizing operational efficiency across all aviation layers offers a near-term pathway to accommodate rising demand while reducing environmental impact without waiting for new aircraft technologies.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Digitizing borders could free up space to reimagine #PaxEx

Border control and lost baggage are the two remaining “pain points” when it comes to creating seamless air passenger journeys. But smart technology deployment and closer cooperation between airlines, airports and regulators could help resolve these issues and free up enough space to transform the...

Modernizing border digitization could reclaim physical airport space currently devoted to security queues, enabling airlines and airports to reinvest terminals in premium passenger amenities and retail experiences.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

How Much Does Pilot Training Cost Before A US Airline First Officer Reaches The Flight Deck?

A closer look at the roadmap to the cockpit.

Aspiring airline pilots face $100,000-$200,000 in cumulative training costs before earning their first officer position, creating a significant barrier to entry that may worsen pilot shortages.

See Simple Flying →

U.S. Considering Deployment of 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East

Senior officials are reportedly considering the deployment of the Immediate Response Force, which includes a Battalion Combat Team of the 82nd Airborne Division, according to U.S. media. After increasing rumors regarding a deployment of U.S. Army troops being in progress, new reports now mention ...

Military airlift capacity will face increased demand if the 82nd Airborne deploys, potentially straining strategic transport resources during concurrent global commitments.

Since 2019, the legacy carrier has added nearly 40% more premium seats.

United's diversified aircraft strategy across three platforms simultaneously increases premium capacity and operational complexity, requiring integrated crew training and maintenance protocols.

How The Fairchild Republic A-10 Warthog’s Titanium Bathtub Protects Pilots From Ground-Level Ballistics

The A-10's titanium armor strategy prioritizes pilot survivability over aircraft survivability, fundamentally shaping close-air-support aircraft design philosophy.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA PC-12 Aircraft Makes Move to Support Flight Research Across Agency

A NASA Pilatus PC-12 aircraft will now be based at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, in order to support flight research efforts across the agency. The PC-12 was acquired in 2022 by NASA’s Glenn Research Center in Cleveland for use in advanced technology development....

NASA's centralized PC-12 deployment to Armstrong reflects a strategic shift toward consolidating research aircraft resources for multi-center collaboration on advanced technology programs.

See NASA →

Delta's suspension of congressional perks signals airlines' willingness to leverage operational disruptions as pressure points against government dysfunction affecting airport operations.

Students entering aerospace careers can gain direct experience with lunar and Mars-bound technologies through Artemis participation, creating a pipeline of talent for sustained space exploration missions.

Chal...
